title: podman pull from yocto
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Try to run `podman pull` inside yocto give this error:
`cannot setresgid: Invalid argument`


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

not so easy to reproduce because you need a yocto

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body:
Are you running podman as root?  If you are not running podman as root, please show the output of `podman info` when you as the unprivileged user as well as the output of `podman --log-level debug unshare true`

Could you please show also the content of `/proc/self/uid_map`?

I'm not running podman as root

Inside the Yocto:

```
> /usr/local/bin/podman --log-level debug unshare true

"/usr/local/bin/podman filtering at log level debug"
"Called unshare.PersistentPreRunE(/usr/local/bin/podman --log-level debug unshare true)"

"Merged system config \"/usr/share/containers/containers.conf\""

"Using conmon: \"/usr/bin/conmon\""

"Initializing boltdb state at /home/meox/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db"

"Using graph driver vfs"
"Using graph root /home/meox/.local/share/containers/storage"
"Using run root /run/user/1000/containers"
"Using static dir /home/meox/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod"
"Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp"
"Using volume path /home/meox/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"

"Set libpod namespace to \"\""
"Not configuring container store"
"Initializing event backend file"

"Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument"
"Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument"
"Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument"
"Using OCI runtime \"/usr/bin/crun\""

cannot setresgid: Invalid argument

Error: setting up the process
```

uid_map from the host machine:
```
 cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0          0 4294967295
```


uid_map from inside yocto:
```
+ cat /proc/self/uid_map
      1000       1000          1
```

so yocto is running in a user namespace where a single user is mapped `1000       1000          1`

With such configuration you are not able to run containers that use IDs != 0.  You'll also need to set `ignore_chown_errors = "true"` in your storage.conf file.

Since the issue depends on your environment, I am going to close the issue but please feel free to comment further

but why podman pull depends by this environment?
dokcer pull is working well
Is Docker itself also running in the yocto user namespace or is running on the host?

> Is Docker itself also running in the yocto user namespace or is running on the host?

both running from yocto: the `docker pull` is issued from yocto but the docker daemon runs in the host

I'm trying to debug what's going on and I found this:

At line 396 of `/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.go` in `becomeRootInUserNS` function 
b[0]: is '1'
but when I run it outside yocto b[0] is '2'

but before to arrive at that line someone (I don't know where) print "cannot setresgid: Invalid argument"

What I really need is a simple way to pull from a docker registry using `podman` and have the my root storage filled correctly.
For "filled correctly" I mean that if from the host I do: `podman images --root=<myroot>` I'm able to see it.

Doesn't the following work?
podman --root=`<myroot>` pull IMAGE
podman  --root=`<myroot>` image list

> podman --root= pull IMAGE

cannot setresgid: Invalid argument

> podman --root= image list

cannot setresgid: Invalid argument

Setting or not setting the root parameters doesn't change the result (unfortunately).
What I'm trying to do is a "simple" pull. I'm not trying to run the container.

If I try to use `podman --remote` it works but I'd like to avoid it

> both running from yocto: the `docker pull` is issued from yocto but the docker daemon runs in the host

yes that is the difference.  Since docker runs in the host then it is not limited by the yocto user namespace.

You could do a similar thing with podman and run it as a daemon in the host (`podman system service -t 0`) and connect to it from the yocto environment.

In the yocto user namespace you've access to only one ID. Assuming we manage to get it working, you'd still be forced to squash all your images to just one ID.

I am having a similar problem.
running on ubuntu 20.04 as build machine.
podman install on this machine is 3.4.2
when running from regular shell podman pull succesful
when running from honister version podman pull succesful
when running same recipe from kirkston version it fails.
see log below. 
addded below the log output of podman info, podman info --debug

can you direct me what to change ?
